correct.label: Correct strategy
correct.description: Make a ten, then preserve the leftover.

frames.caption: Add 2 to make ten: 8 needs 2 more, and 6 has it to give.
8 + 6 = 14: split 6 into 2 and 4, make ten, then 4 left.

grounding.metaphor_gloss: Object Construction — numbers as wholes built from parts

breaks_at.reason: Zero requires the ad-hoc Zero Object Metaphor; lack-of-object is not itself a constructible object.
No source-domain referent for 'an object that is the absence of an object'; requires the ad-hoc Zero Object Metaphor.
Irrationals are not constructible by finite assembly of unit parts.

teacher.embodied: Fit equal parts together into a whole object.
teacher.incompatibility_penumbra: Rules out the make ten drop leftover deformation (dropped leftover after make ten): the representation will not sustain it.
teacher.standard: addition strand: make ten split leftover

incorrect.label: Incorrect strategy
incorrect.description: Make a ten, then drop the leftover.

frames.caption: Add 2 to make ten: 8 needs 2 more, and 6 has it to give.
The ten is full, but the leftover 4 from 6 is dropped. The student keeps 10 instead of preserving the full sum 14.

frame_sequence.caption: Add 2 to make ten: 8 needs 2 more, and 6 has it to give.
8 + 6 = 14: split 6 into 2 and 4, make ten, then 4 left.
Add 2 to make ten: 8 needs 2 more, and 6 has it to give.
The ten is full, but the leftover 4 from 6 is dropped. The student keeps 10 instead of preserving the full sum 14.

correct.label: Correct strategy
correct.description: Decompose one ten and reduce the tens place.

frames.caption: The ones place has 2 but needs 3; borrow one base-group from the tens place (now 12 here), then 12 - 3 = 9.

grounding.metaphor_gloss: Object Construction — numbers as wholes built from parts

breaks_at.reason: Zero requires the ad-hoc Zero Object Metaphor; lack-of-object is not itself a constructible object.
No source-domain referent for 'an object that is the absence of an object'; requires the ad-hoc Zero Object Metaphor.
Irrationals are not constructible by finite assembly of unit parts.

teacher.embodied: Fit equal parts together into a whole object.
teacher.incompatibility_penumbra: Rules out the borrow without reducing bases deformation (unbalanced decomposition exchange): the representation will not sustain it.
teacher.standard: subtraction strand: decompose base for ones

incorrect.label: Incorrect strategy
incorrect.description: Add ten ones but leave the tens place unchanged.

frames.caption: The ones place has 2 but needs 3; make 12 here without reducing the next place (tens). Then 12 - 3 = 9.
Borrowing without reducing the next place gives 29, not 19.

frame_sequence.caption: The ones place has 2 but needs 3; borrow one base-group from the tens place (now 12 here), then 12 - 3 = 9.
The ones place has 2 but needs 3; make 12 here without reducing the next place (tens). Then 12 - 3 = 9.
Borrowing without reducing the next place gives 29, not 19.

correct.label: Correct strategy
correct.description: Use a scalable number-line measure model for the large magnitude.

frames.caption: Start at 204,500 on a scale-broken number line.
Append one jump of +695 to land at 205,195.

grounding.metaphor_gloss: Motion Along a Path — numbers as point-locations reached by moving

doc.note: A bounded number-line length model: the second addend is appended to the first without unrolling unit jumps.

breaks_at.reason: In the source domain of motion, doing something a negative number of times makes no sense; the iterated-motion grounding of multiplication cannot be extended to negative multipliers.

teacher.embodied: Move along a path, away from or back toward a starting point.
teacher.incompatibility_penumbra: Rules out the count all when count on available deformation: the representation will not sustain it.
teacher.repair: Switch to Multiplication by -1 is Rotation by 180 degrees.
teacher.standard: addition strand: count on from larger

incorrect.label: Misconception visual refused
incorrect.description: Base-ten blocks stop being a productive physical vocabulary here; a collapsed-cube picture would be a labeled misconception, not a calculator answer.

doc.error: Representation grammar refused this base-ten picture: reason(no_physical_block_for_required_place_value(204500))
